❀【Install & Adjust Simply】- Screws and screw drivers are free provided to install, very easy to put it on and remove. Free size fits most wrists (5.5"-8.1"/ 139mm-206mm), with plenty of spots for size adjustment.

reviews.title: Perfect replacement
reviews.text: Exact replacement and a fraction of the price you would pay if you bought it from the store

reviews.title: Blue is more purple
reviews.text: The blue is more purple than it is blue. The color was mostly true to its display photo though, but just a word of caution in case if anyone really wanted a true blue. As for the band itself, I can’t say much about durability since I just installed it. This band comes with 2 securing bands whereas the original Garmin comes with 1. I also feel like the band doesn’t meet flush with the watch face as well as the original Garmin but it’s not bad. This band is also a little stiffer than the floppy Garmin band, and I think I actually like the feel of this stiffer band.

reviews.title: Fits 45s perfectly
reviews.text: Worked perfectly as a replacement band for the 45s. Not sure why other reviewers are having issues because it’s great!

reviews.title: I wanted the teal specifically
reviews.text: I replaced my watch band as soon as they came and it's been on since summer with no complaints.  I appreciate the fact that they send a little screwdriver with the set, it's so helpful not having to find something that small on your own.  Nice product!

reviews.title: Easy set up and can text and phone calls
reviews.text: I like the looks and it tells me to get off my ass and move.

reviews.title: Decently priced
reviews.text: Chose this watch because of the simplicity and overall look.  The step counts and sleep features work very well.  Battery life is approximately 2 days use when using the push button feature.  If you enable the sensor to come on with your wrist approximately 1 day use.  Charge works very well and quickly.  The downfall is definitely the rubber banding.  The piece that holds the excess band tends to move.  Call feature is ok for the price and messaging is ok.  I primarily needed for an alarm, clock and date.

reviews.title: great smartwatch
reviews.text: I bought this for my uncle for his birthday. He wanted a smart watch to monitor his health. He liked it and it was easy for him to set up.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.text: I had an Apple Watch before this. This is really no different than the Apple Watch,  everything that worked on there works fine here! Did find a screen protector  it is (Smaat 1.7”) it fits perfect! And touch screen still works fine. as far as I can tell there are no other bands made for this type of connection, which is kind of ridiculous. So if the band breaks, you literally have to buy a new watch don’t like that! Also, wish it could at least have the temperature on the screen! And there are only certain screens that have the power level or day and date, and you cannot pick a screen from your photos, etc. and add the date. Just trying to inform people of all of the things that I was looking for in reviews and no one ever mentioned any of these hopefully it helps!

reviews.title: Nice watch!
reviews.text: I bought this watch to track my fitness mainly.  I'm not the type of person who's into gadgets per se but I think this is a winner.  It's not expensive but still stylish and functional.

title: iTECH Fusion 2 S Smartwatch Fitness Tracker, Heart Rate, Step Counter, Sleep Monitor, Message, IP67 Water Resistant for Men and Women, Touch Screen, Compatible with iPhone and Android (Black Mesh)
description: Looking for a smartwatch that won't break the bank? The iTech Fusion 2 S smartwatch includes high tech features at an affordable price. The iTech Fusion 2 S watch doubles as a fitness tracker - including a calorie counter, pedometer, and a sleep monitor! Receive phone notifications, weather forecasts, and control music right from your wrist! Also includes sports mode such as cycling, baseball, basketball, football, tennis, soccer, golf and walking. So many great features on a full touchscreen dial! Keep up with your busy lifestyle with the iTech Fusion 2 S smartwatch. Complete with convenience, this watch features a motion gesture, so you'll be able to view the screen just by turning your wrist.
features: The iTech Fusion 2 S smartwatch keeps you motivated and on track with your fitness and weightloss goals. Get alerts from the Sedentary Reminder for when it is time to start a new activity, and feel rewarded when you reach your workout goals with the Achievement Notifications!
Monitor the quality of your workouts and sleep patterns with the Calorie Tracker and Sleep Monitor. Assures that you are getting the proper rest to achieve optimum exercise quality.
Advanced Multi-Sports Mode technology records data from your favorite activities, including running, cycling, hiking, basketball, tennis and football.
Pair your iTech Fusion 2 S smart watch with the iTech Wearables app - compatible with most iOS and Android smartphones.
Certified IP67 - Water Resistant technology keeps your watch undamaged rain or shine! Watch is durable for up to 3 feet of water.

reviews.title: Seems to work pretty well for the most part
reviews.text: Setup was easy with a quick app download and it paired with my iPhone 12 without any drama. Biometric tracking seems to work reasonably well with the exception of heart rate, which seems to always read 70 no matter what I'm doing. I'm not too crazy about the aesthetic of the watch, it just looks kind of cheap to me. I got this mainly to use as a health tracking tool and to tell time, not to make and receive calls/texts with, so I haven't setup any of those features yet and may never get around to doing so. So i can't comment on ease of use for any of those features. I did like that there are quite a lot of different watch face designs that can be applied and without any additional purchase. For the price of around $40, this is a pretty smokin' deal for a smart watch, I think.

reviews.title: Not bad for the price
reviews.text:
I’ve tried a few different affordable watches and my biggest issue has been the charging port. They have trouble staying connected with tiny pins and no magnetic pull. So going into this I’m more critical.
Upside is there is definitely a better charging system. It holds in place nicely even though it’s a pretty small pin area it is easier to charge.
The screen does have low sensitivity response. When pairing it it took 3 tries to get it recognize I had tapped the screen.
On a good note it’s reading well and has some great features like heart rate, oxygen, and sleep. Everything lined up well with my normal statistics so I feel it’s pretty accurate.
There’s a variety of images to choose from so you can customize the screen however you would like.
It’s very comfortable. The band holds like a premium watch band. It has a large face that is classy. Aesthetically it’s very nice.
Overall for an affordable smart watch it’s a decent option.

reviews.title: Okay tracker
reviews.text: For the price, it is an okay tracker.  The microphone on the watch is not of great quality as I was told sounded muffled on it.  It does have a lot of features and was quite easy to use but nothing about it made it stand out from the other hundreds of smart watches out on the market.

reviews.title: Cute watch is all I can say
reviews.text: Initially the watch was quick to charge and holds the charge for a couple of days.  The functions were easy to use, and a lot to choose from for the activities I do.  I mainly track my steps, distance, duration, heart rate.  It worked well for a few weeks, then I took it swimming for a few laps in the pool.  That was a mistake, battery stopped working (won't charge anymore).  Sad, I really liked the watch, it's cute but the battery died.

reviews.title: Great Smart watch for the essentials
reviews.text:
I was looking for a good smart watch for my mother. She was a Fitbit user but her Fitbit died after years of use and wanted something with a similar feel.
This watch really worked out for her.
It's got a great screen and a good amount of apps to provide essential uses.
It's able to work with iOS and Android.
It's able to get notifications from apps and make phone calls.
Not a bad product at all.

title: Kimnix Smart Watch 2022(Make Call Receive/Dial), 1.72 in HD Full Touch Screen Fitness Tracker with Bluetooth Call/Text/Heart Rate/SpO2/Sleep Monitor Smartwatch for Android iOS Mens Women (Black)

reviews.title: Fair features for the price
reviews.text:
First of all, you must keep in consideration that this is a $50 watch. That being said I will state what I consider are the pros and cons:

Pros
Price, battery life (more than the typical 1-day of apple watches), capable of answering and dialing within the watch (Bluetooth required) and blood oxygen and heart rate sensors.

Cons
Strap does not hold to watch and you must keep correcting it (solved with another strap?), blood pressure monitor? How???, you only have five watch faces at any given time but you can only replace one of the five - the others are permanently set, you get to edit the fourth one by adding a photo

Overall, there are cheaper and better watches like the WYZE (which I own and has better quality) but lack the phone answering and dialing features.

I will be replacing the strap with another type to see if it holds.

reviews.title: It does not keep all of the apps current. That is what I bought it for.
reviews.text: I bought this watch per my doctors request to keep health issues.  I only use it for this feature.  But to my amazement, It does not do as it states.  As I use only the medical aspects only I would expect it to work perfectly.

reviews.title: A good knock off
reviews.text: For the money this is a good watch

🦄 EXPRESS YOURSELF: These gadgets aren't just great for teaching; they also allow kids to express their unique personalities. Choose from 10+ unique band designs and watch faces! PTHTECHUS Smartwatches are the perfect gifts for kids of all ages!

reviews.title: Best gift ever
reviews.text: Bought this for a 9 year old. It’s amazing. It has so many things on it and is easy to use. She shows it to everyone who will look at it and she feels like it’s just like her big brothers and sisters watches. Very happy.

reviews.title: 2G is Dead
reviews.text: This watch says that it utilizes a sim card with 2g service. This service is nearly dead everywhere. The sim card area in the watch is a little tricky to figure out. You have to open the back and then lift up the battery slightly. The sim card slot is located under the battery. You have to slide(left/right) and lift up the metal sim card cover like a door. Insert the sim card and lay the metal cover down over it and slide it into place to lock it. This watch is not waterproof. The watch works as specified except for the 2g calling. 2g service is not available in my area, so make sure you check if your area still has it. You have to buy your own sim card and they recommend Speedtalk service. Check their coverage map for 2g service.

title: Garmin 010-02541-10 fenix 7X Sapphire Solar,Larger adventure smartwatch, Solar Charging Capabilities, rugged outdoor GPS watch, touchscreen, wellness features, Carbon Gray DLC Titanium with Black Band
description: There are 7 days in a week. And the fēnix® 7X Sapphire Solar multisport GPS watch is built to go strong for all of them. Featuring a big 1.4” display, built-in LED flashlight and Power Sapphire™ solar charging lens, it has the long-lasting battery life to sustain your athletic and outdoor challenges. Use trusted button controls or the new touchscreen interface to access selections. Gain performance insights and metrics that help you manage your exertion and build stamina. Optimize recovery with 24/7 health and wellness monitoring of heart rate, respiration, stress, sleep and more this device is intended to give an estimation of your activity and metrics; it is not a medical device). Outdoor navigation sensors and new multi-band GNSS technology help guide you with a higher level of positioning accuracy, even in challenging environments. Plus, you’ll have preloaded TopoActive maps and worldwide ski and golf maps right on your wrist. To complement your tamer days, connected features include smart notifications when connected to a compatible smartphone, music storage and Garmin Pay™ contactless payments in supported countries and payment networks.
features: Long-running solar powered multisport GPS watch with scratch-resistant Power Sapphire lens and always-on 1.4” display uses the sun’s energy to extend battery life; a built-in LED flashlight keeps you going after dark.Supported Application:Elevation Tracker,Distance Tracking,Fitness Tracker,Multisport Tracker,Sleep Monitor. Connectivity technology:Wi-Fi,GPS. Wireless comm standard:Bluetooth

Battery performance: in smartwatch mode, up to 28 days while indoors or up to 37 days with solar charging in 3 hours of direct sunlight (50,000 lux) per day; in GPS mode, up to 89 hours indoors or up to 122 hours with solar charging continuously in direct sunlight (50,000 lux)
Take your training to the next level with endurance and real-time stamina tracking, training status, advanced performance metrics and 30 plus built-in sports apps

Get 24/7 health and wellness monitoring with wrist-based heart rate, Pulse Ox (not available in all countries), stress and enhanced sleep tracking (this device is intended to give an estimation of your activity and metrics; it is not a medical device)

Navigate the outdoors with a higher level of positioning accuracy, thanks to new multi-band frequency and GNSS network support (GPS, GLONASS and Galileo) plus built-in sensors for 3-axis compass, gyroscope and barometric altimeter
Find freedom and adventure with preloaded TopoActive maps — plus maps for thousands of golf courses and ski resorts worldwide
Built-in Wi-Fi connectivity lets you download maps for any continent at any time — without using a computer

Enjoy everyday lifestyle connectivity features, including smart notifications when connected to a compatible smartphone, music storage and our Garmin Pay contactless payment solution in supported countries and payment networks
Trusted button controls that work in any environment are matched with a highly responsive touchscreen interface for quick, convenient access to selections

reviews.title: Pricey but worth it for multi day backpacking
reviews.text: I tried the Fénix 5X and returned it because it was very tedious to use the maps with buttons only.  The Fénix 7X Sapphire Solar not only solves this problem but has a massive increase in battery life and better sensors all around.  Most well-traveled trails don’t need fancy GPS watches, but I’ve been going off the trails more and more, and having an accurate GPS and not just a breadcrumb app is convenient. It beats relying only on Compass and maps only.

reviews.title: More watch than I need
reviews.text: I've had the 3 and 6x prior to this. I upgraded for the flash and the touchscreen as well as the significantly improved battery life. This thing is bullet proof. All the previous functionality through the Garmin app is maintained if not improved. I use it as an everyday watch and for my biking and hiking needs. I've also used it for swimming and SUP and have been impressed. As with previous versions, more faces will be developed so I'm not holding the slim options against it for now. Not cheap but it's hands down the winner in this space for me.

reviews.title: Comprehensive and accurate. Excellent fitness tracking device.
reviews.text: I use the Fenix 7 to track all  of my fitness activity, my sleep numbers, weather, text messages, and security around the house, music, etc..  It was too expensive for me but I'm glad I purchased it as it has paid for itself and has become indispensable in many ways.  I cannot imagine a better more functional fitness watch.  You will appreciate the battery life and all the other available functions.

reviews.title: A worthwhile upgrade from the 6X
reviews.text:
I just upgraded from the 6X sapphire to a 7X solar sapphire watch during Garmin's anniversary sale. It wasn't that I was unhappy with the old one, it's been invaluable, but there were just enough new features I decided it'd be worth a try. All-in-all I'm very glad that I did. I weighed the extra battery life from the larger solar array along with the ability to reuse my existing QuickFit 26 bands into my decision to keep the same size rather than go with something a bit smaller and, perhaps, sportier-looking. Fortunately I have big hands and wrists so it doesn't look overlarge.

Others have spoken about the smartwatch in more detail, so I'll both be brief and focus on the upgrade process instead of on the watch itself here. Given Garmin's otherwise clunky software, I was amazed at how easy it was to configure the new device, download a watch face, and port over my data from the old one. Even with the firmware and map upgrades it only took about 20 minutes, most of which was downloading stuff and reconnecting the new device to my wi-fi and phone app. All I needed was Garmin Express (on my laptop) and Garmin Connect (on my smartphone).

The bottom line is that the 7X looks great, works perfectly, has massively improved battery life, is easy to set up, and comes with a few extra cool features like the flashlight (which is far more useful than expected).

reviews.title: good but expenisive
reviews.text:
My old tracking watch was a suunto abit 2. I does the basic recording of your running, cycling, walking and show you the distance on the map when downloaded.

I was looking at a garmin bike gp map, like the 830 series. I just wanted to see streets and location as I bike and not use my cellphone. There aren't that many models that show real-time map, even the one from other brand that shows map, it lacks a lot of details or cross street. Garmon fenix 7 was the most rebus in the lineup in term of map.

Goods (mostly compared to my older suunto ambit 2:
+faster gps acquiring
+has heartbeat detector, so don't have to wear a chest monitor.
+fit nicely, I have skinny wrist and it is almost the same sixe as the height
+More detail on map than the regular fenix 7.
+battery is like around 2 weeks if I don't realy use the recording.
+convenient to have a map on your wrist, grant it is a tad small.
+tell me I have low Vo2.
+can monitor your sleep pattern, rem, deep sleep, etc.
+I was on the east coast and now back on the west coast and I didn't even have to adjust the time. Not sure If I had to adjust the daylight either.
+like google smartwatch it can send you alert on your screen.
+battery is great, since it has solar and doesn't have the bright and vivid oled screen of garmin epic. ( I bought mine instore and was able to compare both) I prefer the longer battery life. My smartphone can be fore more detail items.
+alot of good info, temp, alt, sun set heart rate you can see on the fly.
+touch screen works very well.

neutrals:
*has a flashlight led, I don't use it much. Actually I hard every use it.
*good little vibrate when you get alert.
*this is music and wallet stuff I also don't use.

Negatives:
-cost
-while the map is good, I might want a bigger cycling map later.
-compared to the garmin epic which has a brighter screen, this one isn't as bright, Seem reflect light so you can see even at night but not very bright.
-it is quit dim at times

I want to get the base model but looking at this at the store, i wanted the bigger screen, the solar power and sapphire. I scratched my watch from just bumping into things so anything less than sapphire is a no go. Is still might end up buying a cycling gps map, but this is good enough for now. price is just way too high when they add one feature over the next model for 100 more.

Also I really like the way the metal looks for where it meets the fitbit

reviews.title: Finally a comfortable stretchy band
reviews.text: This watch band, that I have for my FitBit Inspire 2 is just what I was looking for. I hated the band that came with it.  I tried another buckle, cloth band, which was ok, but still a challenge to take on and off of my wrist.  This band is easy to attach to the Fit bit watch, and the length is adjustable (which I think is unusual for a slip-on stretchy band.  I even am ok with the multi color.  It makes it easy to pair up with lots of tops.  It can be adjusted in length to most everyone's wrist.  I have a very small wrist (short woman here) and I was able to make it small enough for me. But there is also a lot of room for expansion.

reviews.title: Very cute

This one is like putting on your cake pants after Thanksgiving Dinner. It’s not the prettiest but it’s just fine. More than fine. You can live (too much?) in this thing…

title: Garmin Forerunner® 955 Solar, GPS Running Smartwatch with Solar Charging Capabilities, Tailored to Triathletes, Long-Lasting Battery, Whitestone
description: Lead the pack with Forerunner® 955 Solar, the premium GPS running and triathlon smartwatch with Power Glass™ solar charging to extend battery life. The training readiness feature tells you when you’re primed for a productive session — while daily suggested workouts adapt to your performance and recovery alongside the race widget, which provides training tips, course details and completion time predictions. With morning report, you can get an overview of your sleep, recovery, training and more — as well as a picture of your overall health with heart rate variability (HRV) status (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics). On the run, new multi-band GPS and full-color, built-in maps help you navigate confidently. And with traditional buttons and a touch display, it has never been easier to tap into your training status and know whether you’re training productively, peaking or strained. If you’re pushing toward the podium — or to the very limits — Forerunner 955 Solar is by your side with up to 20 days of battery life with solar charging in smartwatch mode (assuming all-day wear with 3 hours per day outside in 50,000 lux conditions).
features: Built with an always-on, full-color display that’s light on the wrist and easy to read even in direct sunlight

Battery life: up to 20 days of battery life in smartwatch mode for a full picture of your health — from sleep to training (assuming all-day wear with 3 hours per day outside in 50,000 lux conditions); up to 42 hours in GPS mode and up to 49 hours with solar charging (assuming continuous use for entire period in 50,000 lux conditions); up to 110 hours in UltraTrac mode with solar charging (assuming use in 50,000 lux conditions)
Click or swipe through maps and stats with traditional button controls or a new, responsive touchscreen
Whether you’re running on city streets or riding densely covered trails, Forerunner 955 provides full-color, built-in mapping to keep you on track

Access multiple global navigation satellite systems, and get access to multiple frequencies sent by navigation satellites for improved accuracy in areas where GNSS signals are reflected, weak or typically don't penetrate — giving you superior accuracy for how far and how fast you’ve run — even in challenging environments
From the moment you wake up, get a training readiness score based on your sleep quality, recovery, training and more, so you can determine whether it’s a good day to go hard — or take it easy
Get ready for your next race with race widget — which provides training tips, personalized daily suggested workouts and completion time predictions based on course details, weather and performance

Using new indicators such as HRV status, your recent exercise history and performance, get an easy insight into your overall effort with training status — and know whether you’re training productively, peaking or strained

Receive an overview of your sleep, recovery and training outlook as soon as you wake up with morning report — alongside HRV status, training readiness and weather; you can even customize your report to show what you want to see (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics)

With HRV status, gain a deeper understanding of your overall health, recovery and training performance through heart rate variability while you sleep, based on technology developed by our Firstbeat Analytics team (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics)

reviews.title: A great watch for a runner
reviews.text: Haven't found anything I don't like about this watch.  Have begun following it's training advice, and it appears to be working.  Provides a wealth of interesting information.  One real plus for us old guys: the face is larger than the 735XT making it easier for these old eyes to read it.

reviews.text: Verdaderamente útil, el seguimiento de sueño, entreno, potencia, y muchos mas datos que nos permiten dar un seguimiento más preciso del estado físico.

reviews.title: VO2 and race prediction are garbage
reviews.text:
For some reason with the 955 - Garmin decided to stop updating VO2 and race prediction - so if you have come to rely/look at that - don't get this watch. They have tried fixing it with the last few beta updates, but they can't seem to figure out how to correct it.

Multi-band GPS is nice - which honestly is the only reason we all haven't moved back to the 945/735 - which are AMAZING watches.

reviews.title: Fantastic update
reviews.text:
The 955 is a fantastic update over the previous 9XX, 7XX, and 6XX models. This update I'd much more communicative and provides loads more encouraging updates and workout suggestions. The interface is also more fluid than previous models. The return of touch to the user interface is nice but, optional.

Definitely worth going with

reviews.title: Nice looking but---
reviews.text:
Just received yesterday so can't comment on accuracy yet. Got watch set up through app with no problems. Then the maddening part-One of reasons I ordered this watch was watch face on Amazon pictures. Well, I scrolled down through all the faces on the app 4 times and the shown watch face was not even there for download-What the hell!!!!! I've ordered 6 or 7 smart watches and this is the first one without magnetic charging cable. Has this plastic thingy at end of cord you have to push watch down into in order to charge. Don't like this at all. Haven't decided whether to keep or not but not off to a good start.
After a few days another problem. Battery charge on watch shows 100% all the time even after 2 days of wearing. Impossible for battery level not to go down somewhat if not a lot. Returning.

reviews.title: Cheap watch with premium features!
reviews.text:
I own several smart watches only because I like different styles. I have to say this one is very stylish and elegant. Works very well and has tons of features. The watch band feels strong and durable.

Some of the reading features such as the blood pressure monitor is not 100 percent accurate but pretty close compared to a cuff system.

Battery last a few days before needing a charge again. It’s also IP68, has music control once paired to your phone, heart rate monitor, blood oxygen readings, call & text notifications, and so much more!

If you are looking for a smart watch that would not break the bank, then this is it! Go and get yourself one of these today! It’s highly recommended!

It not only has the function of heart rate, blood pressure and sleep monitoring, but also has sedentary reminders, remote photography, alarm clock, smart stopwatch, call reminder, information synchronization, waterproof, Bluetooth, etc.

❤【Compatible with Android IOS Phones】 The smartwatch supports all kinds of phones on the current market. When the "H Band" APP is installed on your phone, the smartwatch can be connected to the phone via Bluetooth, then data can be synchronized, you can read the content of SMS or SNS (such as Skype, ICQ, Facebook, WhatsApp, Youtube, Viber, LinkedIn, Instagram, Twitter…). The smart watch will remind you when there is an incoming call. You will never miss any important calls or messages.

❤【Fitness and Health Tracker】 The smart bracelet can record daily steps, calories, distance, and record a variety of sports data such as running, climbing, hiking, ball... and provide professional data analysis. In addition, the smart wristband can monitor your heart rate, blood pressure, sleep, and record data in real time. When you work long time in front of your computer, the smart bracelet will remind you to rest. Change a little bit every day, and your health will change a lot.

❤【Tailored for Women】 This ladies smartwatch with spherical tempered glass, exquisite appearance, and 1.08” HD color screen. The women smart watch is designed with female menstrual cycle function, reminds the calculated safe period and fertile period.

❤【Use and Precautions】 This smartwatch can adjust the length of the strap according to the instructions, and the package contains a detailed instructions with English. This smartwatch supports multiple languages. When you receive the smart watch, please check and charge the watch. Then turn on the Bluetooth in your phone and run the “H Band” APP in your phone, pair this smartwatch to your phone from the “H Band”, do not pair directly with your phone’s Bluetooth settings.

reviews.title: So glad I found this one!
reviews.text: I am so happy with the way this looks. It doesn’t look a traditional smart watch. It is more stylish. Would purchase again

reviews.title: Looks great but a pain to navigate
reviews.text: I ordered this watch because I love the style. I don’t care for the styles of most smart watches I’ve seen.  This watch looks great, very stylish!!  It comes with a tool to allow you to adjust the band length which is very helpful.  It keeps time perfectly.  It counts your steps and monitors sleep quality. The heart rate and BP monitors seem to be accurate as they are right on with my normal numbers. It measures sleep quality.  The hours I sleep is accurate but I don't know how to tell if the deep vs. light sleep is correct.  I assume its based on heartrate which seems to be accurate.  I cannot get the female app to sync, however,  I don’t use this feature. The apps are a pain to navigate through but aside from the female app they work well. The best part of this watch aside from its ascetics is that the charge lasts four to five days.

reviews.title: Great Band For Large Wrists
reviews.text: If you have a large wrist (in my case, an arthritic wrist), this is a great product.  Band is well made, sturdy and comfortable. It is very long and is easy to put on.  The only complaint I have is that the dark blue is a lot darker than it appears in the listing.  This could just be that the colors on my iPad screen make it look lighter tha it actually is.  No reason to take off a star.  Great product at a great price.

reviews.title: Solid, Well-made and Fun Band
reviews.text: I purchased this to go with several bands I have some for a black Invicta Pro Diver. The band looks great, was easy to install (obviously, it’s quick release), and looks great. It also came to me quickly. Very satisfied.

title: Garmin Forerunner 245, GPS Running Smartwatch with Advanced Dynamics, Berry
description: You do the running. Forerunner 245 does the thinking. This GPS smartwatch doesn’t just tell you where you ran. It tracks your stats, crunches the numbers and gets to know all about your performance, your running form, your training history and even your goals. All you need to focus on is putting one foot in front of the other.Bezel Material: fiber-reinforced polymer.
features: GPS running smartwatch with advanced training features
Battery life: up to 7 days in smartwatch mode and up to 24 hours in GPS mode
Get free adaptive training plans from Garmin Coach, or create your own custom workouts on our Garmin Connect online fitness community

Provides advanced running dynamics, including ground contact time balance, stride length, vertical ratio and more (When used with Running Dynamics Pod or HRM-Run or HRM-Tri monitors (sold separately))
Safety and tracking features include incident detection (during select activities) which sends your real-time location to emergency contacts through your paired compatible smartphone
Customize with free watch faces, apps and more from our Connect IQ store
Evaluates your current training status to indicate if you’re undertraining or overdoing it; offers additional performance monitoring features

reviews.title: great looking watch and great for running and biking
reviews.text:
I really like this watch. I replaced and old forerunner. (many generation old) It feels great and looks good since you can download and tailor the watch faces. I have downloaded music to it using MediaMonkey and it worked fine. So far, synching with earbuds Jaybird Tarah Pro and Vistas, has been good. The availability of watch faces and activity widgets is nice.
But
Be warned, it takes a little bit to set it up and understand how to understand how to download and update activity widgets. Updating of watch faces isn't too bad. Be patient and read the user guide, there is a lot of functionality you should be aware of whether you use them or not.

reviews.title: Great purchase
reviews.text:
Great, does all of the stuff running nerds love. Spotify is a hassle to sync when i want to adjust my playlists. This is especially annoying for podcast listeners such as myself. For this reason, I could have gone without the music upgrade and just used my phone but I still use the music feature a good amount for my shorter speed runs where I need the running jams!

But obviously I didn’t take a star because Garmin is the GOAT

reviews.title: Great watch
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband, an avid runner, for our anniversary. He is super into tracking, numbers, stats, etc. And he loves this watch! He previously had the Forerunner 235 Garmin but it was probably close to five years old. He had done alot of research on this one verses the newer models and this one still came out ahead and did not disappoint!

reviews.title: great fitness watch!
reviews.text: if you are looking for a smart watch, then this is not for you. however, if you want a fitness watch, then get this one! the gps tracking is great and accurate! it takes a little bit to get used to the buttons and where all the settings are, but i love that i can customize it to do just what i want in terms of display, audio outputs, etc. the band is comfortable and the watch face is a good size to see my stats while working out without being overly cumbersome (i'm a small 5'0" female). i love that it can play my spotify playlists! the only thing of note is that it will not be able to get any data such as weather, sms notifications, calendar updates, emails, phone calls, or send emergency assistance notices unless you have your phone with you and connected via bluetooth. the point of this watch for me was to keep my phone at home so those features don't work during my run, but it does do the fitness tracking and play previously setup spotify playlists, so it's exactly what i want and need. i don't use it for sleep tracking since i only wear it during workouts. love the heart rate monitoring too!

reviews.title: Sleep Tracking and Incoming Call Notification (on android) are EPIC failures
reviews.text:
Overwhelming 5 start reviews really make me skeptical that the reviews themselves are genuine....
Overall the watch is not bad but really needs functional work. The Android Dev/QA team should have some re-training/structuring after releasing this to the general public...
Sleep tracking shows me as sleeping if I am in bed relaxed watching tv during sleep hours, making it useless. I only receive an incoming call notification on the watch if my phone screen is unlocked...
missed calls come in every time..

no problems with GPS, Battery Life, Music , Bluetooth, feeds to 3rd party athletic tracking apps. all those work fine.

Not sure I would buy this again.

title: iTOUCH Explorer 3 Smartwatch (with Heart Rate Tracking, Step Counter, Notifications)
features: HEALTH AND FITNESS TRACKER: The iTouch Explorer 3 smartwatch has a built-in pedometer and a heart rate monitor that automatically tracks your steps, calories burned, cardiovascular activity and sleep. With up to 7 days of battery life!

BONUS FEATURES: Receive notifications for calls, texts, and social media. Daily weather and the 7-day forecast on your wrist. Turn your Explorer 3 into a shutter button with Camera Remote. Control your phone’s music without taking it out of your pocket with Music Remote. Set reminders to drink more water, wash hands, even get up and move around when you’ve been sitting too long. Open our mobile app to track female health, log meals, water intake, weight changes and caffeine consumption.

RUGGED DESIGN: The iTouch Explorer 3 is IP68-rated water resistant with a durable glass screen and a metal bezel to protect your device through general wear and tear. Battery working time: about 7-10 days; Standby time: 30 days.

SPORT MODES: Add another dimension to your fitness with Sports Mode. Choose from a variety of options. Activate to track steps, distance, and calories. Workouts automatically sync with the mobile app so you can look back at your entire history.

FITNESS GOALS: Stay motivated by setting goals in the iTouch Wearables app for steps, calories burned, sleep, water intake, weight and more. Check the app to see all your completed goals. LARGE WATCHFACE LIBRARY: Choose from built-in watch faces, create your own or access the LARGE watch face library in our mobile app! MOBILE PHONE COMPATIBILITY: Connect your Explorer 3 to any Android or iPhone using the iTouch Wearables app*. (*requires iOS 13 or higher and Android 9 or higher)

reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:1dfba93f62e565c5c393181e1d06cded]] What I liked about the ITOUCH EXPLORER 3 SMART WATCH is the very accurate way it keeps track of your health it’s designed with your health in mind it’s easy to use and very easy to setup and stylish and sturdy comfortable wrist band and the battery life is fantastic easy to read dial and it’s a good value for your money it makes a great gift
